column3: and self-contained algorithm implementing **SHADE-E-R (Success-History Adaptive Differential Evolution with Elitist Restarts)**.

column1: 1.  **Elitist Restarts**: Unlike standard restarts that discard all progress
column2: this algorithm carries over the global best solution to the new population (`pop[0] = best_x`). This ensures the algorithm never ""forgets"" the best peak found so far while exploring new areas.

column1: 2.  **Midpoint Bound Handling**: Instead of simple clipping (which causes population bunching at bounds)
column2: variables exceeding bounds are reset to the midpoint between the parent value and the bound. This preserves diversity and search direction near the edges of the search space.

column1: 3.  **L-SHADE Mechanics**: Implements the core self-adaptive parameters ($F$ and $CR$) using historical memory and an external archive to maintain diversity
column2: allowing the use of the greedy `current-to-pbest/1` mutation strategy.

column1: 4.  **Robust Stagnation Detection**: Triggers a restart if the population fitness variance collapses (convergence) or if the best solution does not improve for a set number of generations (stagnation)
column2: maximizing the utility of the available time.

column1: 5.  **Time Management**: A strict time budget calculation ensures the algorithm returns the best result immediately when the time limit approaches
column2: checking constraints even within inner loops.
